Fix BPM detection: detect peaks from same AC signal as waveform display

Previous detectBeat() used a separate EMA baseline that diverged from the
buffer-mean used for drawing, so beats visible on screen weren't detected.

New approach uses the same inverted AC = -(irValue - bufferMean) for both
waveform display and beat detection. Simple peak detector:
- Track when AC transitions from rising to falling (passed a peak)
- Only count as a beat if the peak is in the top 30% of the buffer range
- 333ms refractory period prevents double-counting
- BPM = 60000 / ms since last valid peak

If you can see peaks on the waveform, this will detect them.

commit.message:
Replace checkForBeat() with custom detector for cheap clone sensors

The SparkFun checkForBeat() has fixed internal thresholds tuned for
genuine MAX30102 sensors. AEAK AliExpress clones produce weaker signals
that don't reliably trigger it, causing 'Measuring...' to never clear.

Custom detectBeat() uses the actual signal amplitude:
- Slow EMA (alpha=0.99, ~2s TC) tracks the IR baseline
- Inverted AC = baseline - irValue (positive on heartbeat)
- Dynamic threshold = 60% of tracked peak amplitude, so it adapts to
  whatever signal strength this particular sensor produces
- 333ms refractory period prevents double-counting (~180 BPM max)
- Requires threshold >= 20 to suppress noise when no finger present

Also removes unused heartRate.h include.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix BLE: FFE9 (write) is in service FFE5, not FFE0

Inspecting actual GATT attributes revealed the FNB58 splits the
transparent UART across two services:
  - FFE0 (service0019): FFE4 characteristic — notify only
  - FFE5 (service001e): FFE9 characteristic — write only

The previous code created a single service object for FFE0 and failed
to find FFE9 there. Now both services are opened concurrently and
detail-discovery is waited on for both before starting the stream.

commit.message:
Add analysis: regular polytopes in Minkowski 2+1 spacetime

Systematic analysis connecting the repository's stellation work to the
StackExchange discussion on regular polytopes in Minkowski spacetime.

Key findings:
- SE R0-complement swaps sheets of two-sheeted hyperboloid, does NOT
  produce vertices on one-sheeted hyperboloid (corrected from SE framing)
- Stellation depth gradation (k=1,2,3) is the actual mechanism for
  pushing vertices onto the one-sheeted hyperboloid (dS2)
- Infinite vertex figures at spacelike vertices are forced by non-compact
  SO(1,1) stabilizer, not pathological
- Edges of {7/3} tiling cross the light cone: intrinsically Minkowski
- Classification landscape: Categories A-D of regular structures in R^{2,1}

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Simplify waveform: raw buffer + mean subtraction, no online filters

Previous DC-removal filter had float precision issues (accumulator grew
to millions) and int16_t overflow, causing broken display on cheap AEAK
sensors. The 0x7F LED power may also have been too high.

New approach — zero filter state, impossible to break:
- Store raw uint32_t IR values in a 128-sample circular buffer
- At draw time compute the buffer mean (uint64 sum / count) as the
  DC baseline estimate
- Subtract mean from each sample to get AC, then invert (PPG signal
  drops on heartbeat, so inverting gives upward peaks)
- Per-frame min/max auto-scaling of the inverted AC fills the display
- sampleAverage bumped from 4→8 for cleaner signal on cheap sensors
- LED power restored to 0x1F to avoid photodiode saturation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  non-zero-sum (e.g. $10 split 3 ways = $3.33 each = $9.99)

  remainder distribution so splits always sum exactly to the receipt total
- Tighten transaction validation to exact cent equality (no tolerance) in
  both the API (verifyTransactionDetails) and frontend form submission
- Add 5 regression tests for rounding edge cases with zero-tolerance
  verification (every person must have exactly 0¢ remaining)

commit.message:
Phase 5.5: Enable DisplayList rendering path for widgets

Implement backend-agnostic widget rendering by adding DisplayList support
alongside existing direct buffer rendering. This enables widgets to work
with both software rasterization and GPU backends.

Changes:
- Created internal/raster/consumer package (~478 LOC)
  - SoftwareConsumer: renders DisplayLists via CPU rasterization
  - Full support for all 8 command types
  - 15+ tests with 100% pass rate

- Enhanced widgets with DisplayList emission (~184 LOC)
  - Button.RenderToDisplayList(): emits background, shadow, border, text
  - TextInput.RenderToDisplayList(): emits background, border, text, cursor
  - ScrollContainer.RenderToDisplayList(): emits container + children recursively
  - Added emitRectBorder() and emitScrollbar() helpers

- Integration validation
  - TestButtonRenderParity: validates Draw() and RenderToDisplayList() produce equivalent output
  - All widget tests pass with both rendering paths
  - Zero regressions in existing functionality

Architecture Impact:
- Widgets are now renderer-agnostic (can target software or GPU)
- DisplayList abstraction proven for multi-backend support
- Foundation ready for Phase 6 (AMD GPU) backend swapping

Testing:
- All 15+ consumer tests passing
- All 18 widget tests passing
- All 41 Go packages: 100% test pass rate
- 172 Rust tests: 100% pass rate
- Zero complexity regressions (new functions ≤9.3 cyclomatic)

Phase 5.5 Status: ✅ COMPLETE
Next: Phase 6.1 (AMD GPU kernel ioctls)

commit.message:
Rewrite waveform: proper DC filter + MA filter + inversion + per-frame scaling

Based on the approach used by zuoyi001/oximeter_max30102 and other
proven open-source pulse oximeter projects:

1. DC-removal high-pass filter (alpha=0.95) strips the large constant
   IR baseline, leaving only the pulsatile AC component
2. 4-sample moving average smooths high-frequency sensor noise
3. Signal is INVERTED — the PPG signal drops during a heartbeat (more
   blood = more light absorbed = lower reading), so inverting gives
   the familiar upward-peak pulse shape
4. Per-frame auto-scaling finds min/max of the entire circular buffer
   and maps to the display height every frame — waveform always fills
   the screen regardless of signal amplitude
5. LED power increased from 0x1F (6.4mA) to 0x7F (25.4mA) for a
   stronger signal and more reliable beat detection
6. 50-sample warmup period lets the DC filter settle before drawing
7. Circular buffer with proper read ordering (oldest→newest, left→right)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
